findings.message: Root collection SKILL.md has no concrete input/output examples; the Practical Guidance section describes approach but shows no before/after
Agent documentation does not declare a model anywhere; agents without a declared model default unpredictably at runtime
No agent I/O examples showing a user request and the agent's response; script invocation examples are present but these are not interaction examples
No output format specification; the agent doc does not state what format the agent returns its results in

Custom install path is read from user input via `read -p` and passed directly to `mkdir -p` and `cp -r` without validation; a value containing `..` or shell metacharacters could write files outside intended directories
Skill is built around MiniMax M2.1 interleaved thinking without noting this is a third-party model dependency; reduces portability claimed for the collection
Bullet items in 'Patterns to Avoid' section use `*Silent Tool Failure**` (single leading asterisk) instead of `**Silent Tool Failure**`; bold formatting is broken throughout that section
No YAML frontmatter block present; missing required `name` and `description` fields, making this agent invisible to Claude Code skill loaders
No YAML frontmatter block present; missing required `name` and `description` fields, making this index undiscoverable by any frontmatter-dependent toolchain
Index file has no output format specification; as an agent-adjacent file it should document what consumers can expect from listed agents
No YAML frontmatter block present; missing required `name` and `description` fields, making this agent invisible to Claude Code skill loaders
Orchestrator instructions list `writer` and `analyst` as available agents, but no writer-agent/ or analyst-agent/ directories exist; agents/index.md also omits them
No YAML frontmatter block present; missing required `name` and `description` fields, making this agent invisible to Claude Code skill loaders
All five production dependencies use caret (`^`) ranges, allowing automatic minor and patch upgrades; supply-chain compromise via dependency update is possible
No `## Examples` section with concrete input/output pairs; practical guidance and gotchas are strong but the skill lacks a dedicated worked example
GitHub token embedded directly in git clone URL string (`x-access-token:{token}@github.com/...`); token is visible in OS process listings, shell history, and any logging that captures the command
Examples section contains only one scenario; a second example showing different threshold regimes or a failure mode would improve clarity
Examples section contains only placeholder text (`[describe input]`, `[show expected output]`) rather than a concrete demonstration; template users may copy without replacing
No YAML frontmatter block present; missing required `name` and `description` fields, making this agent invisible to Claude Code skill loaders
No YAML frontmatter block present; missing required `name` and `description` fields, making this agent invisible to Claude Code skill loaders
No YAML frontmatter block present; missing required `name` and `description` fields, making this agent invisible to Claude Code skill loaders
No YAML frontmatter block present; missing required `name` and `description` fields, making this index undiscoverable by any frontmatter-dependent toolchain
Orchestrator instructions list `writer` and `analyst` as available agents, but no writer-agent/ or analyst-agent/ directories exist; agents/index.md also omits them
Agent documentation does not declare a model anywhere; agents without a declared model default unpredictably at runtime
No agent I/O examples showing a user request and the agent's response; script invocation examples are present but these are not interaction examples
No output format specification; the agent doc does not state what format the agent returns its results in
Index file has no output format specification; as an agent-adjacent file it should document what consumers can expect from listed agents
Examples section contains only placeholder text (`[describe input]`, `[show expected output]`) rather than a concrete demonstration; template users may copy without replacing
Root collection SKILL.md has no concrete input/output examples; the Practical Guidance section describes approach but shows no before/after
Skill is built around MiniMax M2.1 interleaved thinking without noting this is a third-party model dependency; reduces portability claimed for the collection
Bullet items in 'Patterns to Avoid' section use `*Silent Tool Failure**` (single leading asterisk) instead of `**Silent Tool Failure**`; bold formatting is broken throughout that section
No `## Examples` section with concrete input/output pairs; practical guidance and gotchas are strong but the skill lacks a dedicated worked example
Examples section contains only one scenario; a second example showing different threshold regimes or a failure mode would improve clarity
GitHub token embedded directly in git clone URL string (`x-access-token:{token}@github.com/...`); token is visible in OS process listings, shell history, and any logging that captures the command
All five production dependencies use caret (`^`) ranges, allowing automatic minor and patch upgrades; supply-chain compromise via dependency update is possible

Custom install path is read from user input via `read -p` and passed directly to `mkdir -p` and `cp -r` without validation; a value containing `..` or shell metacharacters could write files outside intended directories
